channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key U.S. agencies, including the FBI, State Department and the Pentagon, have instructed their employees not to comply with cost-cutting chief Elon Musk's latest demand that federal workers explain what they accomplished last week — or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: They crossed the Darien Gap to reach the US. Now, boat-by-boat, migrants are returning
item.description: They once braved the jungles of the Darien Gap, trekking days along the perilous migrant passage dividing Colombia and Panama with a simple goal: seek asylum in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2,000 USAID staff to be cut, others put on administrative leave
item.description: “There is no one left to do any work,” the USAID official said, requesting anonymity to protect against retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week or face consequences
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his drive to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Columbine survivor Anne Marie Hochhalter dies decades after shooting
item.description: Anne Marie Hochhalter, a survivor of the Columbine High School mass shooting, died Sunday, her former principal, Frank DeAngelis, told Nexstar's KDVR on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KTLA 5

item.title: Universal reveals Shrek, SpongeBob lands for new theme park in Texas
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences recently revealed several themed areas that will be featured at Universal Kids Resort, a new family-focused theme park set to open next year in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KTLA 5

item.title: NY governor makes case for Manhattan tolls in meeting with Trump after feds ordered a halt
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul made the case for Manhattan's congestion tolling during an Oval Office meeting with President Donald Trump after federal officials ordered a halt to the program, a spokesperson for the Democratic governor said.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family says SoCal boy died from dangerous social media challenge
item.description: A Southern California couple is mourning the death of their young son after they say he died from a dangerous social media challenge.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Chaotic scene unfolded as 3 were shot and killed outside Kentucky driver's license office
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Witnesses described a chaotic scene in which three people were shot and killed outside of a Kentucky driver's license office, including one man shot multiple times by masked assailants.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Woman accused of drugging and robbing older men in a deadly romance scheme
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A woman used online dating apps to lure at least four older men to meet her in person, then drugged them with sedatives and stole hundreds of thousands of dollars in a “sinister” romance scheme, FBI officials in Las Vegas said Friday.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL, JESSE BEDAYN and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The justices said in an unsigned order that Hampton Dellinger, head of the Office of Special Counsel, could remain in his job at least until Feb. 26. That’s when a lower-court order temporarily protecting him expires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 3 shot and killed outside Louisville, Kentucky, motor vehicle office, police say
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Three people were shot and killed outside a motor vehicle office in Louisville on Friday, police said.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Fox 5

item.title: You might be unwillingly or unknowingly working with 'terrorist' cartels, report warns
item.description: He warns that with the declaration of drug cartels as "terrorists," any individual or legal entity that may be linked, willingly or unwillingly, to any of the cartels' economic interests is subject to consequences.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alleged ICE harassment of tribal members in New Mexico concern lawmakers
item.description: The vice chairman of the Senate Indian Affair committee and several Democratic colleagues are concerned Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents are conducting unlawful searches and interrogations of tribal members.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol
item.description: Enrique Tarrio, the former national chairman of the Proud Boys, was arrested by U.S. Capitol Police on Friday following a press conference with other Proud Boys members and Oath Keepers’ founder Stewart Rhodes.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4
